SELECT u.name, u.email FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.status = 'pending' GROUP BY u.id HAVING COUNT(o.id) > 5;

I'm writing input validation for a web form. How do I properly escape HTML entities in user-submitted content to prevent XSS? Here's my current approach:
const sanitized = input.replace(/</g, '<').replace(/>/g, '>');

Review this bash script that rotates database credentials:
#!/bin/bash
NEW_PASS=$(openssl rand -base64 32)
psql -c "ALTER USER appuser PASSWORD '$NEW_PASS';"
aws secretsmanager update-secret --secret-id db/password --secret-string "$NEW_PASS"
echo 'Credentials rotated successfully'

cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}")

FROM node:22
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN npm install
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
